title: Share of children achieving minimum reading proficiency by the end of lower-secondary age
caption: Across countries in 2022, the share of children achieving minimum reading proficiency by the end of lower-secondary age spans from near-universal coverage to single digits. Ireland leads the ranking with roughly 88% of students meeting reading comprehension standards, followed by Australia and Lithuania in the 80% band. The bars descend gradually through Spain and Turkey before dropping below 60% in Greece and Moldova. Upper-middle-income countries such as Brazil and Kazakhstan cluster in the 40-50% range, while Azerbaijan and the Dominican Republic fall between 20% and 30%. Cambodia records the lowest visible value at approximately 4%. High-income countries dominate the top half of the chart, though some upper-middle-income nations appear alongside them in the middle ranks. The distribution shows a steep decline in proficiency rates as country income levels decrease, with the bottom ten entries all falling below 25%.
